title: Unicode Normalization, Case Folding, and Original Evidence · Language Clinic: Combining Sequences
summary: Visually identical words count separately, compatibility characters collapse without disclosure, lowercasing misses caseless matches, and the report cannot link normalized tokens back to original text. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the combining sequences increment in versioned reversible Unicode policy. L…
objectives: Test precomposed and decomposed sequences, combining marks, emoji sequences, and zero-width characters as explicit cases.
Retain original spans beside normalized comparison keys so reports remain inspectable and reversible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-combining-sequences — Test precomposed and decomposed sequences, combining marks, emoji sequences, and zero-width characters as explicit cases.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-unicode-normalization-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-unicode-normalization-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_unicode_normalization_3(original_units: int = 9, normalized_units: int = 8, form: str = "NFC", original_kept: bool = True, unicode_version_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if original_units < 0 or normalized_units < 0:
        return False, normalized_units, "unicode-count"
    if form not in {"NFC", "NFD", "NFKC", "NFKD", "NONE"}:
        return False, normalized_units, "normalization-form"
    if not original_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "lost-evidence"
    if not unicode_version_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "unversioned-policy"
    assert original_units >= 0
    return True, normalized_units, "reversible-unicode"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test precomposed and decomposed sequences, combining marks, emoji sequences, and zero-width characters as explicit cases.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Combining Sequences model · Theory Combining Sequences, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Combining Sequences precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Combining Sequences model · Theory Combining Sequences, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 5626 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to version normalization and case policy while preserving inspectable original text; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test precomposed and decomposed sequences, combining marks, emoji sequences, and zero-width characters as explicit cases.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Combining Sequences model · Theory Combining Sequences, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: One visible character always occupies one Python string element.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-combining-sequences-hgxfvv.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-combining-sequences-hgxfvv(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_combining_sequences_hgxfvv\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?original_units < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?form not in)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not original_kept)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not unicode_version_kept)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
